# alche 24-Month P&L Model -- Complete Explanation Guide

**Version:** v3 (Excel file: `alche-pnl-24mo-v3.xlsx`)
**Generated by:** `build_excel_pnl_v3.py` (~1,600 lines, openpyxl)
**Model period:** Month 1 through Month 24
**Funding basis:** EUR 500K pre-seed at EUR 2.5M valuation cap
**All amounts:** EUR, net of VAT unless otherwise stated

---

## How This Document Works

This guide walks through every sheet in the 7-tab Excel workbook. For each line item, it explains what the number is, where it comes from, why it matters, and what happens if you change the underlying assumption. The goal is to make the model fully transparent -- for the founders who built it, and for any investor who opens the file and wants to understand the logic.

Throughout this document:
- **Assumptions!B##** refers to a specific cell on the Assumptions sheet that drives the calculation.
- **Growth Curves row ##** refers to the monthly forecast data that feeds unit volumes into the P&L.
- **D#** references are decisions (D1 through D11) documented on the Decisions sheet.
- **P#** references are persona audit findings (P1 through P7) from seven expert reviews of the model.
- **R#** references are research documents (R1 through R10) that validated specific assumptions.

---

## Sheet 1: P&L (183 rows, v3.3)

This is the master financial statement. It reads from the Assumptions sheet (for rates and prices) and Growth Curves sheet (for unit volumes), then calculates everything using Excel formulas. Change an assumption or a growth curve number, and the entire P&L recalculates automatically.

### Structure Overview

The P&L flows top-to-bottom in a standard format:

```
Subscriber Metrics (context, not financial)
  |
Income (6 revenue streams + 2 deliberately zeroed lines)
  |
- COGS (11 variable cost lines)
  |
= Gross Profit
  |
- Operating Expenses (payroll, R&D, S&M, G&A, management, travel, one-off costs; Growth Marketer removed)
  |
- Contingency Buffer (10%)
  |
PROFITABILITY BRIDGE (Income → COGS → Gross Profit → OpEx → EBITDA with margins)
  |
= EBITDA
  |
- CapEx (16 one-time investments incl. Ablöse)
  |
- Depreciation (linear 36-month)
  |
= EBIT (EBITDA − Depreciation & Amortization)
  |
= Net P&L Before Tax
  |
- Tax Provision (~30% when profitable)
  |
= Net P&L After Tax
  |
Cumulative Cash (running total starting from EUR 500K)
  |
Key Metrics (burn rate, ARPU, runway, etc.)
  |
Break-Even Indicators (EBITDA, Net P&L, Cash > EUR 500K)
```

---

### Section: Subscriber Metrics (Rows 5-12)

These rows do not represent revenue. They show subscriber growth dynamics so you can see the engine that drives subscription revenue and AI API costs.

| Row | Line Item | Source | What It Means |
|-----|-----------|--------|---------------|
| New Subs Added | = Growth Curves row 15 | How many new subscribers join each month. M1-M3 = 0 (no app yet), starting 15/mo at M4 launch, ramping to 38/mo by M19-24. Driven by Assumptions!B14-B19. |
| Churned Subs | = Growth Curves row 16 | Subscribers lost each month. Formula: previous month's total subscribers x 8% churn rate (Assumptions!B8). The 8% monthly churn is locked data reflecting industry benchmarks -- 77% of health app users churn by Day 3, and 44% cancel within 90 days. |
| Total Subscribers | = Growth Curves row 17 | Running total: previous total - churned + new. This is the active subscriber base at end of month. Peaks at ~339 by M24. |
| Core (52%) | = Total Subs x Assumptions!B9 | EUR 19/mo tier subscribers. Largest segment by headcount. |
| Pro (38%) | = Total Subs x Assumptions!B10 | EUR 49/mo tier subscribers. The "sweet spot" tier. |
| Premium (10%) | = Total Subs x Assumptions!B11 | EUR 99/mo tier subscribers. Calculated as remainder (Total - Core - Pro) to avoid rounding errors. |

**Why it matters:** The subscriber model is the foundation of the subscription MRR line, the AI API cost line, and the ARPU calculations. Changing churn by even 1 percentage point significantly shifts the total subscriber base by M24.

**Sensitivity note:** Churn rate (B8) is the single most sensitive subscriber metric. At 8% monthly churn, a subscriber stays roughly 12.5 months on average (1/0.08). Drop churn to 6% and average lifetime jumps to ~16.7 months, increasing LTV by 33%.

---

### Section: Income

The model has six active revenue streams plus two deliberately zeroed lines (CGM and Restaurant) kept visible for investor transparency.

#### Retail Products (D11: EUR 49 validated, COGS EUR 23.50)

| Row | Line Item | Formula | Source |
|-----|-----------|---------|--------|
| Units sold | = Growth Curves row 4 | Hardcoded monthly forecast: 0 units in M1-3 (pre-space), ramping from 50 in M4 to 4,600 in M24. |
| Price/unit | = Assumptions!B22 | EUR 49 per unit. Validated by R10 research across 20+ wellness brands. Weighted average of curated premium mix (BRAINEFFECT, Ancient+Brave, Hifas da Terra, Avea) lands at EUR 45-55. |
| Revenue | = Units x Price | Simple multiplication. M4 starts at EUR 2,450; M24 reaches EUR 225,400. |

**Research basis (R10):** The EUR 49 price was validated against real wholesale brand data. BRAINEFFECT FOCUS retails at EUR 29, Hifas da Terra Mico-Leo at EUR 50+, Avea Booster at EUR 95. A curated mix targeting the EUR 45-55 range is realistic for a premium Berlin wellness space.

**COGS correction (D11):** The original model assumed EUR 11.50 COGS (76.5% margin). R10 research showed this was unrealistic -- standard wholesale is 40-60% of retail. COGS was corrected to EUR 23.50 (midpoint of EUR 22-25 range), giving a 52% gross margin. This is still healthy for curated retail but dramatically more honest.

**Open flag (P1/P5):** 4,600 units/month at M24 with only 339 subscribers means ~92.6% of potion sales come from non-subscribers (walk-in retail, e-commerce, potential B2B wholesale). This volume was flagged as "extremely aggressive" by both the Angel Investor persona (P1) and the Growth CFO persona (P5). The model works but the volume assumption needs a clear channel breakdown to be credible.

#### LED Therapy (Free Amenity)

| Row | Line Item | Formula | Source |
|-----|-----------|---------|--------|
| Sessions | = Growth Curves row 5 | 0 in M1-3, 10 in M4, ramping to 300/mo by M20 (capped there). |
| Price/session | = Assumptions!B24 | EUR 0 (free with smoothie purchase). |
| Revenue | = Sessions x Price | EUR 0 in all months. |

**What this is:** Self-service red light therapy (MITO LIGHT panels) offered free to smoothie customers. Customers put the panels on their faces and hang out — no practitioner needed. B24 = 0, B25 = 0.

**Why free:** LED therapy is a dwell-time and foot traffic play, not a revenue line. Offering it free with smoothie purchase increases average visit duration and drives retail product discovery. The EUR 5K CapEx for panels pays for itself through increased smoothie attachment rate and retail exposure. No practitioner cost eliminates the EUR 25/session COGS that existed in the previous model.

#### Smoothie Bar

| Row | Line Item | Formula | Source |
|-----|-----------|---------|--------|
| Units sold | = Growth Curves row 6 | 0 in M1-3, 100 in M4, ramping to 2,000 in M24. |
| Price/smoothie | = Assumptions!B26 | EUR 9.67 average price (R2: Cost Analysis). |
| Revenue | = ROUND(Units x Price, 0) | M4: EUR 967; M24: EUR 19,340. |

**Research basis (R2):** The smoothie bar was costed at EUR 9,022/month base cost with 65% gross margin per drink. Break-even is approximately 45 drinks per day at EUR 9.67 average price. COGS rate is 35% of revenue (ingredients + packaging), set in Assumptions!B27.

#### Community Events

| Row | Line Item | Formula | Source |
|-----|-----------|---------|--------|
| Event count | = Growth Curves row 7 | 0 in M1-3, starting at 1/mo in M4, reaching 5/mo by M20. |
| Avg attendees | = Growth Curves row 8 | Starting at 12 per event, growing to 25. |
| Total attendees | = Count x Avg attendees | Multiplied together. |
| Price/ticket | = Assumptions!B28 | EUR 35 per ticket. |
| Revenue | = Total attendees x Price | M4: EUR 420; M24: EUR 4,375. |

**COGS structure:** Events have two cost components: a fixed venue cost per event (EUR 250, Assumptions!B29) and a ticketing platform fee of 7% (Assumptions!B30, Luma/Eventbrite). The COGS formula is: (Event Count x EUR 250) + (Event Revenue x 7%).

#### Doctor SaaS (D1: EUR 99/mo)

| Row | Line Item | Formula | Source |
|-----|-----------|---------|--------|
| Clinics listed | = Growth Curves row 9 | 0 in M1-4, 2 in M6, growing to 20 (cap) by M15. |
| Fee/month | = Assumptions!B31 | EUR 99 per clinic per month. |
| Revenue | = Clinics x Fee | M6: EUR 198; M24: EUR 1,980. |

**Decision D1 (critical):** The original model had doctors paying EUR 150/mo with a commission model. Research R3 revealed that commission-based referral fees are illegal in Germany under StGB sections 299a/299b (anti-corruption in healthcare). The model was switched to a SaaS platform fee of EUR 99/mo, following the Doctolib precedent (EUR 139-475/mo) and the BGH ruling of February 2025.

**COGS:** Each new doctor costs EUR 25 for verification (Assumptions!B32). This one-time cost appears in COGS using the "New Doctors (delta)" row from Growth Curves row 12, which calculates the month-over-month change in clinic count.

#### CGM Hardware (D3: Removed -- Software-Only BYOD)

This row is deliberately greyed out with EUR 0 across all 24 months. It exists solely for investor transparency. The formatting uses italic light-grey text on a muted background.

**Decision D3:** ZOE dropped CGM hardware in September 2025; Supersapiens shut down in February 2024. The hardware-as-a-service model has been abandoned industry-wide. alche uses a software-only BYOD (bring your own device) approach via Dexcom OAuth 2.0 API and Apple Health/Google Health Connect. This yields 80-90% margins on software interpretation versus thin or negative margins on hardware logistics.

**Why it is visible:** An investor reviewing the model might ask "where is CGM?" -- seeing a greyed-out row with a clear note communicates that this was a deliberate strategic decision, not an oversight.

#### Restaurant Revenue (D2: Phase 2 -- EUR 0)

Another deliberately zeroed line. EUR 0 across all 24 months.

**Decision D2:** No health app has successfully charged restaurants commission. This feature is retained as a retention tool for M1-12 but generates zero revenue. It may become a revenue line post-500 users in Phase 2.

#### Subscriptions

| Row | Line Item | Formula | Source |
|-----|-----------|---------|--------|
| Core (EUR 19/mo) | = Growth Curves row 18 x Assumptions!B5 | 52% of subscribers at EUR 19/mo. |
| Pro (EUR 49/mo) | = Growth Curves row 19 x Assumptions!B6 | 38% of subscribers at EUR 49/mo. |
| Premium (EUR 99/mo) | = Growth Curves row 20 x Assumptions!B7 | 10% of subscribers at EUR 99/mo. |
| Subscription MRR | = Core + Pro + Premium | Total monthly recurring revenue from subscriptions. |

**P5 finding (critical):** The Growth CFO persona identified that subscription ARPU is actually EUR 38.44 (not EUR 49 as might be assumed from the Pro tier price), because the tier mix is weighted toward Core. More importantly, subscriptions represent only ~4.7% of total revenue at M24. This means alche is economically a product commerce business, not a subscription business. This has significant implications for valuation methodology -- product commerce businesses trade at 2-4x revenue, while subscription businesses can command 8-15x ARR.

#### TOTAL INCOME

The sum of all revenue streams (Retail + Smoothie + Events + Doctor SaaS + Subscriptions). LED Therapy is included in the formula but contributes EUR 0 (free amenity). CGM and Restaurant are also EUR 0.

---

### Section: Cost of Goods Sold (COGS)

COGS includes 11 variable cost lines that scale with revenue or unit volumes.

| Line Item | Formula Logic | Assumptions Cell | Notes |
|-----------|---------------|-----------------|-------|
| **Product cost (EUR 23.50/unit)** | = Retail Units x B23 | B23 = 23.50 | R10 validated. Was EUR 11.50, corrected to EUR 23.50. Single largest COGS line by M24. |
| **Waste/spoilage (5.5%)** | = Retail Revenue x B36 | B36 = 5.5% | D7: Blended rate -- capsules 4%, powders 7%. Minor line (EUR 50-200/mo early on). |
| **3PL fulfillment (EUR 2.50/unit)** | = IF(M>=4, Units x B37, 0) | B37 = 2.50 | Third-party logistics. Only starts M4 when physical space opens. |
| **LED COGS (free — no practitioner)** | = LED Sessions x B25 | B25 = 0 | Self-service. No practitioner needed — EUR 0 COGS. |
| **Smoothie ingredients + packaging (35%)** | = Smoothie Revenue x B27 | B27 = 35% | R2 validated. 65% gross margin on smoothies. |
| **Events & Ticketing** | = (Event Count x B29) + (Event Revenue x B30) | B29 = 250, B30 = 7% | Fixed venue cost plus platform fee per event. |
| **Doctor Verification (EUR 25/new)** | = New Doctors x B32 | B32 = 25 | One-time cost per new doctor, using Growth Curves row 12 (delta). |
| **AI API -- User-Facing (EUR 0.35/sub)** | = Total Subscribers x B39 | B39 = 0.35 | D6/R9: Hybrid Claude Sonnet 4.5 for blood panels + Gemini Flash 2.0 for routine queries. |
| **Refund/Returns Reserve (1.5%)** | = (Retail + Smoothie + Event Revenue) x B40 | B40 = 1.5% | Applied to physical product and event revenue only. |
| **Bad Debt Provision (0.5%)** | = Total Income x B41 | B41 = 0.5% | P5 recommendation. Applied to total revenue. |
| **Stripe Processing (2.9%)** | = Total Income x B38 | B38 = 2.9% | Payment processing fee on all revenue. |

**AI API cost detail (R9):** The EUR 0.35/subscriber/month comes from a detailed hybrid model: Claude Sonnet 4.5 handles blood panel interpretation (1x/month, ~EUR 0.036/interaction) because it has 54% diagnostic accuracy versus Gemini's 34%. Gemini Flash 2.0/2.5 handles daily insights, Q&A, meal recommendations, and behavior nudges (~9-14x/month) at EUR 0.0001-0.006/interaction. The EUR 0.35 includes a 20% buffer for edge cases. At 339 subscribers (M24), this costs approximately EUR 119/month -- a negligible line item.

### Gross Profit and Gross Margin

**Gross Profit** = Total Income - Total COGS

**Gross Margin %** = Gross Profit / Total Income (shown as percentage, with a guard against division by zero in months with no revenue).

The blended gross margin should land in the 45-55% range once all streams are active. The retail product stream (52% margin) is the highest-volume line but not the highest-margin. Subscriptions approach ~99% margin (only COGS is the AI API cost). LED therapy is free with no COGS — it contributes EUR 0 revenue and EUR 0 cost.

---

### Section: Operating Expenses (OpEx)

OpEx is organized into seven subsections plus a 10% contingency buffer.

#### Payroll (7 lines)

| Line Item | Formula | Start Month | Assumptions Cells |
|-----------|---------|-------------|-------------------|
| **Timu (CEO)** | = B44 x B45 | M1 | B44 = EUR 4,166/mo (EUR 50K/yr), B45 = 1.25x multiplier |
| **Daria (COO)** | = B44 x B45 | M1 | Same as CEO. Both founders at EUR 50K gross. |
| **Space Staff** | = IF(M>=4, B46 x B45, 0) | M4 | B46 = EUR 1,230/mo (Midijob ~25hrs/wk). |
| **Partner Manager** | = IF(M>=6, B47 x B45, 0) | M6 | B47 = EUR 2,000/mo. |
| **CTO** | = IF(M>=18, B48 x B45, 0) | M18 | B48 = EUR 6,500/mo (EUR 78K/yr). P7 flagged this as below 25th percentile (Glassdoor EUR 80-187K range). |
| **BG Insurance (0.5%)** | = Sum of all payroll x B49 | M1 | B49 = 0.5%. Berufsgenossenschaft statutory accident insurance. P7 corrected from 1.5% to 0.5% for office work. |
| **KSK Levy (4.9%)** | = Growth Curves row 11 x B50 | M1 | B50 = 4.9%. Kunstlersozialkasse levy on creative freelancer payments. P2 requirement -- mandatory when engaging designers, writers, etc. |
| **Payroll Total** | Sum of all 7 lines above | | |

**Employer multiplier (P2/P7):** The 1.25x multiplier converts gross salary to total employer cost. It covers: employer's share of health insurance, pension, unemployment insurance (Arbeitgeberanteil), plus U1/U2/U3 Umlagen. Updated from 1.22x to 1.25x based on persona research.

**Growth Marketer removed (Timu review):** The Growth Marketer hire (EUR 4,500/mo from M12) was removed because Timu (CEO) handles growth marketing himself. This saves EUR 73,125 over the 24-month model (EUR 5,625/mo fully loaded x 13 months M12-M24). Marketing ad spend budget in Growth Curves row 10 is unchanged -- that's media budget, not a person.

**Hiring timeline:** The staggered hiring creates a step function in monthly costs. M1-3: two founders only (~EUR 10,415/mo). M4: add space staff. M6: add partner manager. M18: add CTO (largest single hire). Each new hire also increases BG insurance proportionally.

#### R&D (2 lines)

| Line Item | Formula | Assumptions Cell | Notes |
|-----------|---------|-----------------|-------|
| **App Maintenance** | = B56 | B56 = EUR 1,500/mo | External agency retainer for ongoing development. |
| **R&D Total** | = App Maintenance | | EUR 1,500/mo flat. |

**UX/UI moved to CapEx (Timu review):** The UX/UI design retainer (EUR 800/mo) was removed from R&D OpEx and capitalized as a one-time EUR 15,000 "App Design & Development" CapEx item in M1 (Assumptions!B108). This reflects the reality that UX/UI is an app build cost, not an ongoing operational expense. The EUR 1,500/mo App Maintenance retainer covers ongoing dev agency work post-launch. Net impact: saves EUR 19,200 in OpEx over 24 months, adds EUR 15,000 to M1 CapEx.

#### Sales & Marketing (4 lines)

| Line Item | Formula | Assumptions Cell | Notes |
|-----------|---------|-----------------|-------|
| **Paid Ads** | = Growth Curves row 10 | N/A (hardcoded in Growth Curves) | Starts at EUR 50/mo (M1), ramps to EUR 3,000/mo (M24). This drives the CAC calculation. |
| **Content Creation** | = B86 | B86 = EUR 150/mo | Canva, stock imagery, tools. |
| **Influencer Seeding (M7+)** | = IF(M>=7, B87, 0) | B87 = EUR 200/mo | Product seeding to influencers. |
| **S&M Total** | Sum of 3 lines | | |

**CAC note (P1):** Customer acquisition cost (Paid Ads / New Subs) starts very low in beta (EUR 17 at M1) and rises as the business scales. P1 flagged that a EUR 40 blended CAC is aggressive versus industry benchmarks of EUR 70-120 for health/wellness apps.

#### General & Administrative (23 lines)

This is the largest OpEx section, expanded significantly by persona audits. Every line item was validated by one or more expert reviews.

**Rent & Space Operations:**

| Line Item | Formula | Assumptions Cell | Notes |
|-----------|---------|-----------------|-------|
| **Rent (co-work M1-3 / space M4+)** | = IF(M<=3, B61, B62) | B61 = 750, B62 = 2,500 | Co-working in M1-3, then Kaltmiete for physical space. |
| **Nebenkosten M4+ (P3)** | = IF(M>=4, B63, 0) | B63 = 325 | P3 finding: Rent is Kaltmiete only. Must add EUR 4-5/sqm for Nebenkosten (heating, water, building insurance, property tax). |
| **Utilities M4+ (Strom/Gas/Wasser)** | = IF(M>=4, B70, 0) | B70 = 500 | P3: increased from EUR 400 to EUR 500 for a commercial food-service space. |
| **Cleaning/Maintenance M4+** | = IF(M>=4, B71, 0) | B71 = 300 | P3: daily cleaning requirement for food-service space. |
| **Maintenance Reserve M4+** | = IF(M>=4, B72, 0) | B72 = 200 | P3: equipment repair fund. |
| **Waste Disposal M4+ (P3)** | = IF(M>=4, B73, 0) | B73 = 120 | P3: BSR commercial waste collection. |
| **WiFi/Internet M4+** | = IF(M>=4, B74, 0) | B74 = 50 | P3: business fiber connection. |
| **POS System M4+** | = IF(M>=4, B75, 0) | B75 = 50 | Cash register with TSE (technical security equipment, mandatory in Germany). |

**Professional Services:**

| Line Item | Formula | Assumptions Cell | Notes |
|-----------|---------|-----------------|-------|
| **Steuerberater** | = B64 | B64 = 400 | Monthly tax advisor. Runs from M1. |
| **Bank Fees (P5)** | = B94 | B94 = 35 | P5: Geschaftskonto monthly fee. |

**Insurance (P6 -- split into 5 categories):**

The original model had a single EUR 200/mo insurance line. P6 (Compliance Legal persona) identified this as critically insufficient. The model now carries five separate insurance lines totaling EUR 650/mo:

| Line Item | Formula | Assumptions Cell | Notes |
|-----------|---------|-----------------|-------|
| **General Liability** | = B65 | B65 = 200 | Betriebshaftpflicht. Runs from M1. |
| **D&O Insurance** | = B66 | B66 = 100 | P6: mandatory with angel investors on the cap table. |
| **Cyber Insurance** | = B67 | B67 = 150 | P6: processing health data under Art. 9 GDPR creates significant data breach liability. |
| **Product Liability** | = B68 | B68 = 100 | P6: selling dietary supplements creates product liability exposure. |
| **Product Recall M4+** | = IF(M>=4, B69, 0) | B69 = 100 | P4: only needed once retail products are on shelves. |

**Regulatory & Compliance (P2/P4/P6):**

These lines were almost entirely missing from earlier model versions. Seven persona reviews identified them as mandatory for a Berlin GmbH handling health data and selling food/supplements:

| Line Item | Formula | Assumptions Cell | Notes |
|-----------|---------|-----------------|-------|
| **DSB / ext. DPO** | = B76 | B76 = 400 | P6: MANDATORY under Art. 37 GDPR. External data protection officer. Required because alche processes special category health data. |
| **CMP Cookie Tool** | = B77 | B77 = 25 | P6: TDDDG (Telekommunikation-Digitale-Dienste-Datenschutz-Gesetz) requires cookie consent management. |
| **Food Safety Compliance M4+** | = IF(M>=4, B78, 0) | B78 = 80 | P4: ongoing HACCP monitoring and food safety documentation. |
| **IHK Berlin** | = B79 | B79 = 10 | P2: Chamber of Commerce membership. EUR 120/year prorated. P2 corrected from EUR 300/yr to EUR 120/yr. |
| **GEMA M4+** | = IF(M>=4, B80, 0) | B80 = 31 | P2: music licensing for the physical space. EUR 370/year prorated. |
| **Rundfunkbeitrag** | = B81 | B81 = 6 | P2: mandatory broadcasting fee for businesses, EUR 6.12/mo for 0-8 employees. |
| **Verpackungsgesetz** | = B82 | B82 = 6 | P2: LUCID registration and dual system participation for packaging waste. EUR ~72/year prorated. |

**One-off:**

| Line Item | Formula | Notes |
|-----------|---------|-------|
| **Permits & Licenses (M3)** | = IF(M=3, 500, 0) | Pre-opening permits. Hardcoded at EUR 500 in M3. |

**G&A Total:** Sum of all 23 lines above.

#### Management & APIs (3 lines)

| Line Item | Formula | Assumptions Cell | Notes |
|-----------|---------|-----------------|-------|
| **SaaS Stack (D9)** | = IF(M<=6, B58, IF(M<=12, B59, B60)) | B58=338, B59=500, B60=750 | D9: Itemized stack. Phase 1: Google Workspace + Figma + Notion + Crisp. Phase 2: adds tools. Phase 3: full stack. PostHog free (replaces Mixpanel), Terra deferred (D8). |
| **Claude Max (internal)** | = B57 | B57 = 210 | D6: internal AI budget for the team. Separate from user-facing AI API (which is in COGS). |
| **Management Total** | = SaaS + Claude | | |

#### Travel (4 lines)

| Line Item | Formula | Assumptions Cell | Notes |
|-----------|---------|-----------------|-------|
| **Investor Travel** | = B88 | B88 = 150 | Monthly. |
| **Conferences M7+** | = IF(M>=7, B89, 0) | B89 = 100 | Industry events, starting M7. |
| **Local Transport** | = B90 | B90 = 50 | BVG monthly tickets. |
| **Travel Total** | Sum of 3 lines | | |

#### One-Off People Costs

| Line Item | Formula | Assumptions Cell | Notes |
|-----------|---------|-----------------|-------|
| **Recruitment -- CTO (M17)** | = IF(M=17, B51, 0) | B51 = 18,000 | P7: headhunter fee at 20-30% of salary. Original model had EUR 5K; corrected to EUR 18K. |
| **CTO Onboarding (M18)** | = IF(M=18, B52, 0) | B52 = 3,500 | P7: equipment (laptop, monitors, peripherals) plus setup costs. |

#### Total OpEx Structure

```
OpEx (pre-contingency) = Payroll + R&D + S&M + G&A + Management + Travel + Recruitment + Onboarding
Contingency Buffer = OpEx (pre-contingency) x 10% (Assumptions!B93)
TOTAL OPEX = OpEx (pre-contingency) + Contingency Buffer
```

The 10% contingency buffer is applied to all operating expenses before it. This is a standard practice for pre-seed models to account for unexpected costs.

---

### Section: Profitability Bridge

The Profitability Bridge provides a step-by-step waterfall from revenue to EBITDA, making the margin structure transparent at a glance. EBITDA stands for **Earnings Before Interest, Tax, Depreciation & Amortization**.

| Row | Line Item | Formula | What It Shows |
|-----|-----------|---------|---------------|
| Note | "EBITDA = Earnings Before Interest, Tax, Depreciation & Amortization" | — | Definition for readers unfamiliar with the term |
| Total Income | = Total Income row | Reference | Starting point for the waterfall |
| − Total COGS | = −Total COGS row | Negative reference | Variable costs subtracted |
| = Gross Profit | = Gross Profit row | Reference | Revenue after direct costs |
| Gross Margin % | = Gross Profit / Total Income | Percentage | Should land in 45-55% range once all streams active |
| − Total OpEx | = −Total OpEx row | Negative reference | Fixed and semi-fixed costs subtracted |
| **= EBITDA** | = Gross Profit − Total OpEx | **Grand total** | Operating profitability before CapEx, depreciation, and taxes |
| EBITDA Margin % | = EBITDA / Total Income | Percentage | Operating margin at the EBITDA level |

**Why this section exists:** EBITDA is the primary operating profitability metric investors look at for early-stage businesses. The Profitability Bridge makes it easy to see exactly where margin is created (Gross Profit) and consumed (OpEx). The key milestone is when EBITDA turns positive -- that signals the business is covering its ongoing costs from revenue.

---

### Section: Capital Expenditures (CapEx)

CapEx represents one-time investments that build the business infrastructure. These are split into M1 items (company and legal setup) and M3 items (physical space buildout).

**M1 Items (Company Formation and Legal/Regulatory Setup):**

| Line Item | Amount | Assumptions Cell | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-----------------|-------|
| GmbH Formation | EUR 2,500 | B102 | Notary fees and Handelsregister registration. |
| Legal Opinion HWG | EUR 3,500 | B103 | Legal opinion on Heilmittelwerbegesetz compliance (D3/R3). |
| R&D Stability Testing | EUR 5,000 | B104 | Testing supplement formulations for shelf stability. |
| DPIA | EUR 7,500 | B105 | P6: Data Protection Impact Assessment. MANDATORY under Art. 35 GDPR for health data processing. |
| MDR Classification Opinion | EUR 7,500 | B106 | P6: Medical Device Regulation classification. Needed to confirm the app does not fall under MDR scope. |
| Privacy Policy + ToS | EUR 5,000 | B107 | P6: Legally compliant privacy policy and terms of service under Art. 13/14 GDPR. |
| App Design & Development | EUR 15,000 | B108 | UX/UI capitalized as app build cost (moved from monthly OpEx). |
| **M1 Total** | **EUR 46,000** | | |

**M3 Items (Physical Space Buildout):**

| Line Item | Amount | Assumptions Cell | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-----------------|-------|
| Space Buildout | EUR 25,000 | B109 | P3: increased from EUR 15K. Covers construction, plumbing modifications, electrical, interior fit-out. |
| Kitchen Equipment | EUR 20,000 | B110 | Commercial-grade smoothie bar: blenders, refrigeration, prep surfaces, sinks. |
| LED Therapy Devices | EUR 5,000 | B111 | MITO LIGHT panels for red light therapy room. |
| Fire Safety Equipment | EUR 300 | B112 | P3: extinguishers and signage. |
| Signage & Branding | EUR 500 | B113 | P3: window and exterior signage. |
| HACCP Plan Setup | EUR 1,000 | B114 | P4: Food safety consultant to develop HACCP plan. |
| Hygiene Training | EUR 60 | B115 | P4: IfSG section 43 training for 4 persons (EUR 15/person). |
| Food Safety Equipment | EUR 500 | B116 | P4: Temperature monitoring, thermometers, sensors. |
| Ablöse — Key Money | EUR 50,000 | B117 | v3.3: Commercial lease takeover (hava parası). Capitalized intangible asset, amortized over lease term. §5 Abs. 2 EStG. |
| **M3 Total** | **EUR 102,360** | | |

**Total CapEx: ~EUR 148,360** (v3.3: +EUR 50K Ablöse)

**Note:** Kaution (security deposit, EUR 7,500, B118) is NOT CapEx. It is a refundable balance sheet asset that appears as a cash outflow on the Cash Flow sheet only. No P&L impact.

#### Depreciation

Depreciation is calculated as linear depreciation over 36 months (Assumptions!B119).

- M1-2: Only M1 items depreciate. Monthly depreciation = M1 CapEx total / 36.
- M3-24: Both M1 and M3 items depreciate. Monthly depreciation = (M1 total + M3 total) / 36.

Depreciation is a non-cash charge -- it reduces accounting profit but does not consume cash. This distinction is important for the Cash Flow sheet.

#### EBIT (EBITDA − Depreciation & Amortization)

After the depreciation row, two additional rows complete the profitability picture:

| Row | Line Item | Formula | What It Shows |
|-----|-----------|---------|---------------|
| = EBIT | = EBITDA − Depreciation | Total | Earnings Before Interest and Tax. Accounts for CapEx amortization. |
| EBIT Margin % | = EBIT / Total Income | Percentage | Profitability after non-cash depreciation charges. |

**Why EBIT matters:** EBITDA is the cash-oriented profitability metric; EBIT is the accounting-oriented one. EBIT includes the impact of capital investments spread over their useful life (depreciation). For alche, the gap between EBITDA and EBIT reflects the ~EUR 148K in CapEx being amortized over 36 months (~EUR 4,121/mo from M3 onward). Investors focused on accounting profitability (or future P&L for valuation) should look at EBIT.

---

### Section: Net P&L and Tax

**Net P&L (before tax) = EBITDA - Total CapEx**

Note: CapEx here is the cash spent in the month, not the depreciation. This means M1 and M3 show large negative spikes when CapEx is deployed.

#### Tax Provision (P2: ~30% when profitable)

The tax provision was added based on P2 (Steuerberater persona) findings. A Berlin GmbH pays:
- **Gewerbesteuer:** 14.35% (3.5% Steuermesszahl x 410% Berlin Hebesatz). Note: GmbH has NO Gewerbesteuer Freibetrag (unlike sole proprietors).
- **Korperschaftsteuer + Solidaritatszuschlag:** 15.825% (15% KSt x 1.055 Soli).
- **Combined effective rate:** ~30.175%, rounded to 30% (Assumptions!B99).

**Activation logic:** Tax provision is ONLY charged when BOTH conditions are true:
1. Monthly pre-tax P&L is positive (the business made money this month), AND
2. Cumulative pre-tax P&L is positive (lifetime profits exceed lifetime losses).

This means tax does not kick in until the business has recovered all its cumulative losses. This is a simplification of German loss carryforward rules (Verlustvortrag) but is directionally correct for a pre-seed model.

**Formula:** `=IF(AND(monthly_pretax > 0, cumulative_pretax > 0), ROUND(monthly_pretax * 30%, 0), 0)`

#### Net P&L (after tax)

= Net P&L (before tax) - Tax Provision

#### Cumulative Cash

Starting with EUR 500,000 (the pre-seed raise), this row adds/subtracts each month's Net P&L (after tax) to show the running cash balance.

**Formula for M1:** `= 500000 + M1 Net P&L`
**Formula for M2+:** `= Previous month cumulative + Current month Net P&L`

**Important caveat (P5):** This cumulative cash line does NOT account for working capital requirements (inventory that must be purchased before it sells). The true cash position is shown on the Cash Flow sheet. The gap between this line and actual cash can be significant -- P5 estimated ~EUR 218K at M24.

---

### Section: Key Metrics (Bottom of P&L)

| Metric | Formula | What It Tells You |
|--------|---------|-------------------|
| **Monthly Burn (if loss)** | = IF(Net P&L < 0, ABS(Net P&L), 0) | How much cash the business consumes per month during unprofitable periods. |
| **Revenue / Subscriber (Total ARPU)** | = Total Income / Total Subscribers | Total revenue per subscriber including all revenue streams. Misleading because most revenue comes from non-subscriber retail. |
| **Subscription ARPU (P5: actual)** | = Subscription MRR / Total Subscribers | The true subscription yield per subscriber. Expected to be ~EUR 38.44 given the tier mix (52/38/10 at 19/49/99). |
| **Subscription MRR** | = Subscription total from income section | Monthly recurring revenue from subscriptions only. |
| **Subscription % of Total Revenue** | = Subscription MRR / Total Income | P5 flag: this drops to ~4.7% by M24, confirming the business is product-commerce driven. |
| **Gross Margin %** | = references the Gross Margin % row above | Blended gross margin across all streams. |
| **Months of Runway Remaining** | = IF(burning, Cumulative Cash / Monthly Burn, 99) | How many months of cash remain at the current burn rate. Shows 99 if the business is profitable (infinite runway). |

### Section: Break-Even Indicators (Bottom of P&L)

Three flag rows show 1 or 0 for each month, plus two summary rows showing the first month each milestone is hit.

| Row | What It Shows | TOTAL Column |
|-----|--------------|--------------|
| **EBITDA Positive?** | 1 if EBITDA > 0 that month, else 0 | First month number where EBITDA > 0 |
| **Net P&L Positive?** | 1 if Net P&L (after tax) > 0, else 0 | First month number where Net P&L > 0 |
| **Cash > EUR 500K?** | 1 if cumulative cash exceeds the starting EUR 500K raise, else 0 | First month or "Never" |
| **EBITDA Break-Even Month** | Shows "M##" in cell B — the first month EBITDA turns positive | — |
| **Net P&L Break-Even Month** | Shows "M##" in cell B — the first month Net P&L is positive | — |

**Why three measures matter:**
- **EBITDA break-even** = revenue exceeds operating costs (ignores CapEx and tax). This is the operational milestone.
- **Net P&L break-even** = revenue exceeds ALL costs including CapEx depreciation and tax. This is the accounting milestone.
- **Cash > EUR 500K** = the business has generated more cash than it consumed from the raise. This is the investment payback milestone.

EBITDA typically turns positive first (no CapEx drag), then Net P&L follows, and cash recovery comes last (because the P&L cumulative cash line does not reflect working capital drag — see Cash Flow sheet for actual cash).

---

## Sheet 2: Assumptions (125 Parameters)

The Assumptions sheet is the control panel for the entire model. Every value here is referenced by one or more formulas in the P&L, Growth Curves, Cash Flow, Unit Economics, or Scenarios sheets. Change a number here, and every downstream calculation updates automatically.

### Category: Subscription Model (B5-B11)

| Cell | Parameter | Value | Sensitivity | Notes |
|------|-----------|-------|-------------|-------|
| B5 | Core tier price | EUR 19/mo | Low | Entry tier, high volume. |
| B6 | Pro tier price | EUR 49/mo | Medium | "Sweet spot" tier, highest revenue contribution. |
| B7 | Premium tier price | EUR 99/mo | Low | Small subscriber share (10%), but highest per-user value. |
| B8 | Monthly churn rate | 8% | **HIGH** | Single most sensitive assumption for subscriber LTV. Every 1% change shifts average lifetime by ~1.5 months. Locked data based on industry benchmarks. |
| B9 | Core tier share | 52% | Medium | Affects blended ARPU. Higher Core share = lower ARPU. |
| B10 | Pro tier share | 38% | Medium | Affects blended ARPU. |
| B11 | Premium tier share | 10% | Low | Calculated as remainder on Growth Curves sheet. |

### Category: Subscriber Growth (B14-B19)

| Cell | Parameter | Value | Sensitivity | Notes |
|------|-----------|-------|-------------|-------|
| B14 | New subs M1-3 | 0/mo | N/A | No app yet -- subscribers gated to M4+ when space and app launch together. |
| B15 | New subs M4-6 | 15/mo | Medium | Product launch. Physical space and app launch M4. |
| B16 | New subs M7-9 | 22/mo | Medium | Growth acceleration. |
| B17 | New subs M10-12 | 28/mo | Medium | Marketing ramp. |
| B18 | New subs M13-18 | 32/mo | Medium | Scaling phase. |
| B19 | New subs M19-24 | 38/mo | Medium | Scaling phase. |

**What happens if you change these:** Subscriber growth directly affects subscription MRR, AI API COGS, and all unit economics ratios. Doubling M4-6 growth from 15 to 30 would approximately double subscription revenue in that period and increase total subscribers at M24. However, subscription revenue is a small fraction of total income, so the P&L impact is moderate.

### Category: Product Pricing (B22-B33)

| Cell | Parameter | Value | Sensitivity | Notes |
|------|-----------|-------|-------------|-------|
| B22 | Retail product price | EUR 49 | **HIGH** | R10 validated. Affects largest revenue line. |
| B23 | Retail COGS/unit | EUR 23.50 | **HIGH** | R10 corrected from 11.50. Every EUR 1 change shifts gross margin by ~2pp on retail. |
| B24 | LED session price | EUR 0 | N/A | Free with smoothie purchase. No longer a revenue stream. |
| B25 | LED practitioner cost | EUR 0 | N/A | Self-service — no practitioner needed. |
| B26 | Smoothie avg price | EUR 9.67 | Medium | R2 validated. |
| B27 | Smoothie COGS rate | 35% | Medium | R2 validated. 65% gross margin. |
| B28 | Event ticket price | EUR 35 | Low | Events are small revenue. |
| B29 | Event fixed cost | EUR 250 | Low | Per-event venue cost. |
| B30 | Ticketing fee | 7% | Low | Luma/Eventbrite platform fee. |
| B31 | Doctor SaaS fee | EUR 99/mo | Low | D1. Small revenue line capped at 20 clinics. |
| B32 | Doctor verify cost | EUR 25 | Very Low | One-time per doctor. |
| B33 | Doctor max clinics | 20 | Very Low | Cap on doctor count. |

### Category: Cost Rates (B36-B41)

| Cell | Parameter | Value | Sensitivity | Notes |
|------|-----------|-------|-------------|-------|
| B36 | Waste blended rate | 5.5% | Low | D7: capsules 4%, powders 7%. |
| B37 | 3PL cost/unit | EUR 2.50 | Medium | Scales with retail volume. |
| B38 | Stripe fee | 2.9% | Medium | Applied to ALL revenue. At EUR 277K/mo revenue (M24), this is ~EUR 8K/mo. |
| B39 | AI API/user/mo | EUR 0.35 | Low | D6/R9. Tiny cost per user. |
| B40 | Refund reserve | 1.5% | Low | Applied to physical product + event revenue. |
| B41 | Bad debt provision | 0.5% | Low | P5. Applied to total revenue. |

### Category: Payroll (B44-B53)

| Cell | Parameter | Value | Sensitivity | Notes |
|------|-----------|-------|-------------|-------|
| B44 | Founder gross salary | EUR 4,166 | Medium | EUR 50K/yr. P7: viable at pre-seed. |
| B45 | Employer multiplier | 1.25x | **HIGH** | Applied to EVERY salary. 0.01 change affects every payroll line. P2/P7 corrected from 1.22x. |
| B46 | Space staff gross | EUR 1,230 | Low | Midijob ~25 hrs/wk. |
| B47 | Partner Mgr gross | EUR 2,000 | Low | |
| B48 | CTO gross | EUR 6,500 | **HIGH** | EUR 78K/yr. P7 flagged as below market (25th percentile). If raised to EUR 80K+ (EUR 6,667+/mo), OpEx increases by EUR 200+/mo from M18. |
| B49 | BG insurance rate | 0.5% | Very Low | P7 corrected from 1.5%. |

*Note: Growth Marketer (EUR 4,500/mo from M12) was removed. Timu handles growth marketing. Saves EUR 73,125 over 24 months.*

### Category: Fixed Operating Costs (B56-B82)

This is the largest parameter group with 27 line items (UX/UI removed, moved to CapEx). The most sensitive are:

| Cell | Parameter | Value | Sensitivity | Notes |
|------|-----------|-------|-------------|-------|
| B62 | Rent (physical space) | EUR 2,500 | **HIGH** | Kaltmiete. Runs from M4 for 21 months = EUR 52,500. Any increase directly hits bottom line. |
| B76 | DSB (ext. DPO) | EUR 400 | Medium | P6: mandatory. EUR 9,600 over 24 months. |
| B65-B69 | Insurance (5 lines) | EUR 650 total | Medium | P6: runs from M1 (most lines). EUR 15,600 over 24 months. |
| B70 | Utilities | EUR 500 | Low | P3. Only from M4. |
| B56 | App maintenance | EUR 1,500 | Medium | Flat fee, runs all 24 months = EUR 36,000 total. |

Full mapping of all 28 fixed cost cells is in the Assumptions sheet itself, with notes explaining each line.

### Category: Sales & Marketing (B86-B90)

| Cell | Parameter | Value | Sensitivity |
|------|-----------|-------|-------------|
| B86 | Content creation | EUR 150/mo | Low |
| B87 | Influencer seeding | EUR 200/mo | Low |
| B88 | Investor travel | EUR 150/mo | Low |
| B89 | Conferences | EUR 100/mo | Low |
| B90 | Local transport | EUR 50/mo | Very Low |

Marketing spend is primarily driven by the Paid Ads line in Growth Curves (row 10), not by these fixed costs. The paid ads budget ramps from EUR 50/mo to EUR 3,000/mo and is the primary driver of CAC.

### Category: Buffers (B93-B94)

| Cell | Parameter | Value | Sensitivity |
|------|-----------|-------|-------------|
| B93 | Contingency buffer rate | 10% | **HIGH** | Applied to ALL OpEx. At M24, if OpEx is ~EUR 30K, contingency adds EUR 3K. Over 24 months this could total EUR 40-60K. |
| B94 | Bank fees | EUR 35/mo | Very Low | |

### Category: Tax (B97-B99)

| Cell | Parameter | Value | Notes |
|------|-----------|-------|-------|
| B97 | Gewerbesteuer (Berlin) | 14.35% | 3.5% Messzahl x 410% Hebesatz. GmbH pays from first euro (no Freibetrag). |
| B98 | KSt + Soli | 15.825% | 15% Korperschaftsteuer + 5.5% Solidaritatszuschlag. |
| B99 | Combined simplified | 30% | Used in the tax provision formula. Conservative rounding of ~30.175%. |

### Category: CapEx (B102-B119)

16 individual capital expenditure items plus the Kaution (B118, cash flow only) and depreciation period (B119, 36 months). See the CapEx section of Sheet 1 above for full detail on each item.

**Most sensitive CapEx items:**
- B117 (Ablöse — Key Money, EUR 50,000) -- single largest capital outlay. Commercial lease takeover.
- B109 (Space Buildout, EUR 25,000) -- second largest.
- B110 (Kitchen Equipment, EUR 20,000) -- third largest.
- B108 (App Design & Development, EUR 15,000) -- UX/UI capitalized from OpEx.
- B105/B106 (DPIA + MDR, EUR 15,000 combined) -- mandatory regulatory costs that cannot be deferred.

**Not CapEx but related:**
- B118 (Kaution — Security Deposit, EUR 7,500) -- refundable deposit, Cash Flow sheet only. Not P&L.

### Category: Scenario Multipliers (B121-B125)

| Cell | Parameter | Value | Notes |
|------|-----------|-------|-------|
| B121 | Bull revenue multiplier | 1.3x | 30% higher revenue. |
| B122 | Bull churn multiplier | 0.75x | 25% lower churn (6% instead of 8%). |
| B123 | Bear revenue multiplier | 0.6x | 40% lower revenue. |
| B124 | Bear churn multiplier | 1.5x | 50% higher churn (12% instead of 8%). |
| B125 | Bear CAC multiplier | 2.0x | Double the customer acquisition cost. |

---

## Sheet 3: Growth Curves

This sheet contains the monthly volume forecasts that feed into the P&L. Think of it as the "physics" of the business -- how many units, sessions, events, and subscribers exist in each month.

### Unit Forecast Curves (Rows 4-11)

All hardcoded values that you can edit directly:

| Row | Metric | M1-3 | M4 | M12 | M24 | Story |
|-----|--------|------|----|-----|-----|-------|
| 4 | **Retail Units** | 0 | 50 | 900 | 4,600 | No product sales before space opens. Ramps aggressively post-M4. P1/P5 flagged M24 volume as aggressive. |
| 5 | **LED Sessions** | 0 | 10 | 140 | 300 | Free with smoothie. Self-service. Caps at 300/mo (M20+). Tracks usage for foot traffic metrics, not revenue. |
| 6 | **Smoothie Units** | 0 | 100 | 850 | 2,000 | Roughly 45-95 per business day by M24. Break-even is ~45 drinks/day (R2). |
| 7 | **Event Count** | 0 | 1 | 3 | 5 | Community events per month. |
| 8 | **Event Avg Attendees** | 0 | 12 | 20 | 25 | Average attendance per event. |
| 9 | **Doctor Clinics** | 0 | 0 | 17 | 20 | Starts M6, caps at 20 (B33). |
| 10 | **Marketing Spend (EUR)** | 50 | 200 | 1,400 | 3,000 | Paid ads budget. Drives CAC on Unit Economics sheet. |
| 11 | **KSK-Eligible Freelancer Spend** | 500 | 500 | 800 | 800 | Basis for KSK levy calculation (4.9%). |

**Row 12: New Doctors (delta)** -- formula-driven: `=MAX(0, CurrentMonth Clinics - PreviousMonth Clinics)`. This feeds the doctor verification COGS line.

### Subscriber Model (Rows 14-20)

This is the heart of the subscription business model, entirely formula-driven:

**Row 15: New Subs Rate** -- references Assumptions!B14-B19 based on which month it is. Uses nested IF statements to assign the correct growth rate per phase.

**Row 16: Churned Subs** -- `=ROUND(PreviousMonth TotalSubs x Assumptions!B8, 0)`. Churn is applied to the previous month's total (not the current month's, to avoid circular references).

**Row 17: Total Subscribers** -- `=MAX(0, PreviousTotal - CurrentChurn + CurrentNew)`. The MAX(0,...) prevents negative subscriber counts.

**Rows 18-20: Tier Breakdown**
- Core (52%): `=ROUND(TotalSubs x Assumptions!B9, 0)`
- Pro (38%): `=ROUND(TotalSubs x Assumptions!B10, 0)`
- Premium (10%): `=MAX(0, TotalSubs - Core - Pro)` -- takes the remainder to avoid rounding errors where Core + Pro + Premium might not exactly equal Total.

### The Growth Story

**Months 1-3 (Pre-launch):** Zero subscribers -- the app doesn't launch until M4, alongside the physical space. No product sales, no events. The company is in setup mode: GmbH formation, legal/regulatory work, app design and development (EUR 15K CapEx in M1). In M3, EUR 102K CapEx deploys (space buildout, kitchen, LED, Ablöse) plus EUR 7.5K Kaution (cash outflow only).

**Month 4 (Physical space + app launch):** Everything changes simultaneously. The space opens with smoothie bar, free LED therapy (self-service), and retail products. The app launches and subscriber growth begins at 15/mo. First retail sales (50 units), first smoothies (100), first LED sessions (10, free with smoothie), first event (12 attendees).

**Months 4-6 (Early traction):** Revenue ramps from multiple streams simultaneously. Doctor SaaS starts M6 with 2 clinics. Partner manager joins M6.

**Months 7-12 (Growth acceleration):** Subscriber growth increases to 22/mo then 28/mo. Retail units reach 900/mo by M12. Free LED sessions hit 140/mo (drives smoothie attachment). Marketing spend ramps to EUR 1,400/mo.

**Months 13-18 (Scaling):** Subscriber growth at 32/mo. Retail units push past 1,000-2,400/mo range. Doctor clinics approach the cap of 20. CTO recruited at M17 (EUR 18K fee), onboarded at M18 (EUR 3.5K equipment).

**Months 19-24 (Full team, mature growth):** With the CTO on board and all systems firing, subscriber growth reaches 38/mo. Retail units push to 4,600/mo. All revenue streams are at or near their modeled maximums.

---

## Sheet 4: Cash Flow

This sheet exists because of a critical finding from P5 (Growth CFO persona): **P&L profit does not equal cash in the bank.** The estimated gap is approximately EUR 218K at M24, primarily driven by working capital tied up in retail product inventory.

### Why Cash Flow Differs from P&L

Three main reasons:

1. **Working Capital (Inventory):** alche must purchase retail inventory before it sells. The model assumes a 2-month inventory pipeline (you need to have 2 months of expected sales in stock to avoid stockouts). When retail volume is growing, you are constantly buying more inventory than you are selling. This cash goes out the door but does not appear as a cost on the P&L until the product is sold.

2. **Depreciation (Non-Cash):** The P&L charges depreciation monthly as an expense, but the actual cash left the business when the CapEx was purchased (M1 and M3). Depreciation must be added back to P&L profit to get operating cash flow.

3. **CapEx Timing:** Large cash outflows in M1 (EUR 46K) and M3 (EUR 102K CapEx) create sharp dips in cash that the P&L smooths out through depreciation.

4. **Security Deposits:** The Kaution (EUR 7,500 in M3) is a cash outflow that never hits the P&L. It is a refundable balance sheet asset returned at lease end.

### Sheet Structure

**Operating Activities:**
- Net P&L (after tax) -- from P&L sheet
- + Depreciation (add back non-cash charge)
- - Working Capital Build (inventory) = cash tied up in new inventory

The working capital formula: `-ROUND(MAX(0, (CurrentMonth Retail Units - PreviousMonth Retail Units) x COGS per unit x 2 months pipeline), 0)`. When retail volume is growing (which it is in every month from M4 to M24), this is a negative number representing cash consumed by inventory growth.

**Investing Activities:**
- CapEx (negative, representing cash spent on capital items)
- Kaution — Security Deposit (EUR 7,500 in M3 -- refundable, not P&L)

**Financing Activities:**
- Pre-seed funding (EUR 500,000 in M1)

**Net Cash Flow** = Operating + Investing + Financing

**Cumulative Cash Balance** = Running sum of Net Cash Flow. This is the actual cash in the bank account, more accurate than the P&L's "Cumulative Cash" line.

### P&L vs Cash Gap Analysis

The bottom section compares:
- P&L Cumulative Cash (from the P&L sheet)
- Actual Cash (from this Cash Flow sheet)
- GAP = Actual - P&L

The gap is always negative (actual cash is less than P&L suggests) because the P&L does not account for cash tied up in inventory. The gap grows as retail volume grows, reaching approximately EUR -218K by M24.

**What this means for investors:** The EUR 500K raise needs to cover not just operating losses during the ramp-up but also the working capital needed to stock inventory. If cash dips below zero on this sheet, the raise is insufficient.

---

## Sheet 5: Unit Economics

This dashboard gives investors the metrics they use to evaluate early-stage businesses: how much it costs to acquire a customer, how much that customer is worth, and the margin structure of each revenue stream.

### Subscription Metrics

| Metric | Formula | What Investors Look For |
|--------|---------|------------------------|
| **Subscription ARPU** | = Subscription MRR / Total Subscribers | Shows the blended monthly value per subscriber. With a 52/38/10 tier split at 19/49/99, this is approximately EUR 38.44. This is the ACTUAL subscription value, not the headline EUR 49 Pro price. |
| **Subscription LTV** | = ARPU / Churn Rate | At EUR 38.44 ARPU and 8% monthly churn, LTV = EUR 38.44 / 0.08 = ~EUR 480. This is the expected lifetime revenue from one subscriber. |
| **CAC** | = Paid Ads (Growth Curves row 10) / New Subs (Growth Curves row 15) | Customer acquisition cost. Varies by month depending on ad spend and growth phase. |
| **LTV:CAC Ratio** | = LTV / CAC | Investors want to see 3:1 or higher. P1 flagged that a ratio above 5:1 is unusual and may indicate the model is underestimating CAC. The model shows ratios in the 5-15x range, which should be presented conservatively. |
| **CAC Payback (months)** | = CAC / Subscription ARPU | How many months to recover the cost of acquiring one subscriber from subscription revenue alone. |

### Contribution Margin by Stream

Each revenue stream's margin after direct costs:

| Stream | Formula | Expected Margin |
|--------|---------|-----------------|
| **Retail** | = (Revenue - Product COGS - Waste - 3PL) / Revenue | ~42-45% after all direct costs. The EUR 23.50 COGS plus waste (5.5%) and 3PL (EUR 2.50/unit) eat into the 52% headline margin. |
| **LED Therapy** | N/A (free amenity) | N/A. EUR 0 revenue, EUR 0 COGS. Free with smoothie purchase, self-service. |
| **Smoothie Bar** | = (Revenue - Ingredients) / Revenue | ~65% (35% COGS rate). Highest margin physical product. |
| **Subscriptions** | = (MRR - AI API Cost) / MRR | ~99%. Digital product with negligible variable cost (EUR 0.35/user AI API). |

### Revenue Mix

Shows what percentage of total revenue comes from each stream, tracked monthly.

**P5 critical finding (updated for LED-free model):** By M24, the revenue mix is approximately:
- Retail Products: ~85% (dominant)
- Smoothie Bar: ~7%
- Subscriptions: ~5%
- Events: ~2%
- Doctor SaaS: ~1%
- LED Therapy: 0% (free amenity, not a revenue stream)

This means alche is a product commerce business with a subscription wrapper, not a subscription business with physical products. The distinction matters for valuation -- product commerce businesses are valued at 2-4x revenue, while pure subscription businesses can achieve 8-15x ARR multiples. When pitching to investors, frame the subscription as the retention and data engine that powers the higher-margin product recommendations, not as the primary revenue driver.

---

## Sheet 6: Scenarios (Base / Bull / Bear)

This sheet applies simple multipliers to the base case to show best-case and worst-case outcomes. It references the P&L totals (column Z, which is the 24-month sum) and applies the multipliers from Assumptions!B121-B125.

### Scenario Definitions

| Parameter | Base | Bull | Bear |
|-----------|------|------|------|
| Revenue multiplier | 1.0x | 1.3x (B121) | 0.6x (B123) |
| Churn multiplier | 1.0x | 0.75x (B122) | 1.5x (B124) |
| CAC multiplier | 1.0x | 1.0x | 2.0x (B125) |
| OpEx | As modeled | As modeled | As modeled |
| CapEx | As modeled | As modeled | As modeled |

**Important:** The Bull and Bear scenarios change revenue and COGS proportionally but keep OpEx and CapEx constant. This is a simplification -- in reality, higher revenue might require more staff earlier, and lower revenue might mean delaying hires.

### Metrics Shown

| Metric | What It Shows |
|--------|---------------|
| Total Revenue (24-mo) | Sum of all income over 24 months, multiplied by scenario factor. |
| Total COGS (24-mo) | Scales with revenue (since most COGS are variable). |
| Gross Profit (24-mo) | Revenue minus COGS. |
| Total OpEx (24-mo) | Same across all scenarios (fixed cost base). |
| Total CapEx (24-mo) | Same across all scenarios. |
| EBITDA (24-mo) | Gross Profit minus OpEx. |
| Net P&L (24-mo, pre-tax) | EBITDA minus CapEx. |
| Estimated Cash at M24 | = EUR 500K + Net P&L. |

### Key Questions the Scenarios Answer

**Bull case (1.3x):** What happens if growth exceeds expectations by 30%? Cash at M24 increases significantly, but P5's working capital concern becomes more acute -- higher retail volume requires more inventory investment.

**Bear case (0.6x):** What happens if growth comes in 40% below plan? The key question is whether cash goes negative. If Bear case M24 cash is below zero, the EUR 500K raise is insufficient even for the pessimistic scenario. This signals the need to either raise EUR 600-650K (as P1 suggested) or reduce scope.

**OpEx unchanged across scenarios:** This is deliberate. It shows that the business has a fixed cost base that must be covered regardless of revenue performance. In the bear case, this fixed cost base becomes the primary risk -- revenue may not cover operations.

---

## Sheet 7: Decisions

This is a reference sheet, not a financial calculation sheet. It documents the 11 strategic decisions (D1-D11) and 7 persona audit results (P1-P7) that shaped the model.

### Decision Log (D1-D11)

Each decision includes: the choice made, what the previous assumption was, and the P&L impact. Key highlights:

- **D1:** Doctor revenue switched from commission (illegal) to SaaS (EUR 99/mo). Revenue dropped significantly but the model is now legally sound.
- **D3:** CGM hardware removed entirely. Software-only BYOD model.
- **D4:** Physical space added with smoothie bar, LED therapy (free with smoothie), events, retail. This is the core of the business model.
- **D6:** AI costs modeled as hybrid Claude+Gemini at EUR 0.35/user/month (internal validated separately at EUR 210/month flat).
- **D11:** Retail COGS corrected from EUR 11.50 to EUR 23.50 based on actual wholesale pricing research. Margin dropped from 76% to 52%.

### Persona Audit Results (P1-P7)

Seven expert personas reviewed the model. Their combined impact was to add EUR 113-236K in previously missing costs, split across:
- Monthly recurring costs (~EUR 1,600-4,700/mo added)
- One-off setup costs (~EUR 29-53K added to CapEx)
- Tax provision (~EUR 3K/mo when profitable)
- Working capital gap (~EUR 218K at M24)

---

## The Story of the Numbers: A 24-Month Narrative

### Months 1-3: Pre-Launch / Beta (EUR 500K in the bank, burning ~EUR 20-30K/mo)

The company forms as a GmbH in M1. EUR 46,000 in CapEx goes out immediately: legal setup, DPIA, MDR classification, privacy policy, stability testing, app design & development. The two founders draw EUR 4,166/mo each (EUR 50K/yr gross, multiplied by 1.25x employer costs = EUR 10,415/mo total payroll).

Monthly costs are relatively light: co-working rent at EUR 750, app maintenance at EUR 1,500, various insurance and compliance costs. Total monthly burn is approximately EUR 16-18K.

Revenue is zero. No subscribers (app not yet launched), no physical products, no space, no events.

By end of M3, the EUR 25,000 space buildout begins, plus EUR 20,000 in kitchen equipment, EUR 5,000 in LED devices, and EUR 50,000 in Ablöse (key money / hava parası for the commercial lease takeover). Another EUR 102K in CapEx leaves the bank, plus EUR 7,500 for the Kaution (security deposit — refundable, not P&L). Cumulative cash has dropped from EUR 500K to approximately EUR 300-320K.

### Month 4: The Inflection Point

The physical space opens. Overnight, the business goes from a digital-only beta to a multi-stream operation:
- 50 retail product units sold (EUR 2,450 revenue)
- 10 LED therapy sessions (free with smoothie — foot traffic driver)
- 100 smoothies (EUR 967)
- 1 community event with 12 attendees (EUR 420)
- First full month of space rent (EUR 2,500 + EUR 325 Nebenkosten)
- Space staff hired (EUR 1,230 gross)
- Utilities kick in (EUR 500)
- Food safety, waste disposal, GEMA, cleaning costs begin

Revenue in M4: approximately EUR 4,500. But costs jump significantly with the space. The business is still burning cash, but now it has revenue traction.

### Months 4-6: Early Traction

Revenue grows each month as all streams ramp:
- Retail units: 50 -> 100 -> 150
- LED sessions (free): 10 -> 20 -> 35
- Smoothies: 100 -> 150 -> 250
- Partner manager joins M6 (EUR 2,000 gross)
- First doctor clinics listed M6 (2 clinics x EUR 99 = EUR 198)

Monthly revenue by M6: approximately EUR 10-15K. Monthly costs: approximately EUR 25-30K (including contingency). Still burning, but the gap is closing.

### Months 7-12: Growth Acceleration, Approaching Break-Even

This is the critical ramp period:
- Retail units surge from 250 to 900/month
- Free LED sessions reach 140/month (drives smoothie and retail sales)
- Subscriber growth accelerates from 22/mo to 28/mo
- Marketing spend increases from EUR 800 to EUR 1,400/mo
- Influencer seeding begins at EUR 200/mo (M7)
- Conference travel starts (EUR 100/mo)

By M10-M11, monthly revenue approaches EUR 50-70K while costs plateau around EUR 30-35K. The business approaches operational break-even.

**Month 12:** The business approaches operational break-even. Timu handles growth marketing directly (no separate hire), keeping costs controlled while revenue ramps.

### Months 13-18: Scaling and Sustained Profitability

Revenue is now consistently exceeding costs:
- Retail units: 1,100 -> 2,400/month
- Monthly revenue: EUR 80-150K range
- Subscriber base approaches 200-250
- Doctor clinics near the 20-clinic cap
- SaaS stack upgrades to full suite (EUR 750/mo)

**Month 17:** EUR 18,000 CTO recruitment fee hits the P&L. This is a one-off spike in costs.

**Month 18:** CTO joins at EUR 6,500/mo gross (EUR 8,125 fully loaded). EUR 3,500 onboarding cost. Total payroll now includes 5 people: 2 founders, space staff, partner manager, CTO. Monthly payroll approaches EUR 23-25K fully loaded.

Tax provision activates once cumulative pre-tax P&L turns positive. The ~30% rate takes a meaningful bite out of monthly profits, but the business is generating enough revenue to absorb it.

### Months 19-24: Full Team, Strong Growth, Tax Impact

All systems firing:
- Retail units: 2,700 -> 4,600/month (this is the aggressively flagged figure)
- Free LED sessions plateau at 300/month (capacity constrained, drives smoothie attachment)
- Smoothies reach 2,000/month
- 5 events/month with 25 attendees each
- 20 doctor clinics (capped)
- 339 subscribers generating ~EUR 13K/mo in subscription MRR
- Scaling phase: 38 new subs/month

**Month 24 snapshot:**
- Monthly revenue: approximately EUR 264K (LED therapy is now free)
- Retail products dominate at ~85% of revenue
- EBITDA strongly positive
- Tax provision active (~30% on monthly profits)
- Cumulative cash (P&L basis): approximately EUR 595K

**But the Cash Flow sheet tells a different story:** Working capital for retail inventory has consumed approximately EUR 218K over the 24 months. Actual cash in the bank is approximately EUR 377K (EUR 595K - EUR 218K). Still healthy, but significantly less than the P&L headline suggests.

### The Honest Summary

The model shows a business that:
1. Burns through its first EUR 100-130K in months 1-3 on setup and early operations
2. Opens a physical space in M4 that transforms it into a multi-stream revenue machine
3. Approaches break-even around M10-12 as retail product volume scales
4. Becomes sustainably profitable in M13+ despite hiring costs
5. Reaches EUR 277K/mo revenue and EUR 595K cumulative cash (P&L basis) by M24
6. But has approximately EUR 218K less actual cash than the P&L suggests (working capital)

The single biggest risk in the model is retail product volume at M24 (4,600 units/month with only 339 subscribers). This implies 92.6% of retail sales come from non-subscriber channels: walk-in customers at the Berlin space, potential e-commerce, and B2B wholesale. The founders need a clear, credible channel breakdown to make this assumption defensible to investors.

The single biggest financial risk is whether EUR 500K is sufficient, given that the bear case (0.6x revenue) combined with working capital requirements could push actual cash toward zero. P1 recommended raising EUR 600-650K as a buffer. That decision is not reflected in the model -- it still uses EUR 500K.

### What Makes This Model Credible

Despite the flags above, several features make this a strong pre-seed financial model:

- **Every assumption is sourced.** The 125 parameters trace back to 10 research documents, 7 persona audits, and 11 documented decisions.
- **COGS was corrected upward**, not left at the flattering-but-unrealistic EUR 11.50.
- **German-specific costs are modeled granularly**: Gewerbesteuer, Nebenkosten, GEMA, Rundfunkbeitrag, KSK, Verpackungsgesetz, BG insurance, DSB/DPO, IHK. Most pre-seed models ignore these entirely.
- **Illegal revenue models were caught and fixed** (D1: doctor commission -> SaaS).
- **The Cash Flow sheet exists.** Most pre-seed decks pretend P&L profit = cash. This model shows the gap explicitly.
- **CGM hardware removal is visible**, not hidden. Investors can see the deliberate strategic choice.
- **Tax provision follows German rules** with the cumulative loss offset mechanism.

---

| What You Are Looking For | Sheet | Cell/Row |
|--------------------------|-------|----------|
| Monthly subscriber count | Growth Curves | Row 17 |
| Monthly total revenue | P&L | Total Income row |
| Monthly burn rate | P&L | Key Metrics section |
| Cumulative cash (P&L basis) | P&L | Cumulative Cash row |
| Cumulative cash (actual, with working capital) | Cash Flow | Cumulative Cash Balance row |
| Break-even month | P&L | First month where Net P&L > 0 |
| Retail COGS | Assumptions | B23 |
| Churn rate | Assumptions | B8 |
| Tax rate | Assumptions | B99 |
| LTV:CAC ratio | Unit Economics | LTV:CAC Ratio row |
| Revenue mix percentages | Unit Economics | Revenue Mix section |
| Bear case cash at M24 | Scenarios | Row for "Estimated Cash at M24", column D |
